That was (not surprisingly) a great great talk, with a very concrete and live demo, and a good explanations of all the stakes at hand.

Wholeheartedly recommend anyone interested in the matter, attending #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon or not,  to catch the recording afterward!

Not trying to reinvent Kubernetes built-in authorization though: Kyverno juste creates a SubjectAccessReview, fills it with everything retrieved from the request, and ask Kubernetes whether or not it should be allowed (based on RBAC & co).

Lots of MCP parsing (which Kyverno helps with) in order to check the MCP method, caller tool name, etc.

Unmarshal the resourceKind, apiVersion, resourceGroup, etc., and then this is where the real authorization happens.

Moving to the authorization part, where Kyverno shines.
The dangerous part: the MCP tool called uses the primitves of Kubernetes to essentially just apply YAML. And this can go wild, so you want to control that.

Evaluating with the Envoy mode for the Kyverno ValidationPolicy.

Live demo there too - after quite a few at the keynotes, I love that this live demo spirit was never lost at the #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon

And the demo gods were with the speakers; at least until Wi-Fi was required, but they had a proper backup plan. Props and Kudos to them!

Instead of directly accessing our MCP tool, we'll go through the AgentGateway, and AgentGateway will tell user to use OAuth 2.0 flow to authenticate.

Then, user can talk with kagent-tools. And before the actual tool usage, AgentGateway checks with Kyverno whether the user is allowed to or not.

But each MCP implements its own logic, leading to duplication of authz logic.
Interestingly, that's an issue we've been discussing at @payfiteng.bsky.social over the past couple weeks, so I'm super happy to be at this talk!

This is where #Kyverno and the #AgentGateway come in.

recent_posts.text: Ricardo Rocha of CERN and Klaus Ohlmann of Mountain-Wave-Project talk about a glider which uses Kubernetes for “a real-time experiment at the intersection of physics, climate science, and AI”. Amazing presentation at #KubeCon! highaltitudelab.com

"Measuring cosmic rays variation at height."

CERN = systematic WOW use cases. We're so lucky to have such a collaborative and scientific organization around, and moreso to have them use and contribute to open source in such way. 

Love all of it, that was a great great closing #KubeCon keynote !

Crash course on gliding : the #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon is always full of pleasant surprises ! 

Wouldn't have expected otherwise from a keynote Ricardo Rocha is involved in ♥

The project of getting scientific insights from glider flights is really cool and so impressive on the implementation

recent_posts.text:
Excited for #KubeCon + #CloudNativeCon Europe 2026 in Amsterdam  😉

#ONLYOFFICE is proud to join as a silver sponsor, diving into open, sovereign, and self-hosted collaboration tech. Let’s connect, learn, and innovate together! 🤝
The lesson I'm getting from kubecon is that I should be virtualizing my kubernetes so that I can have my AI deploy it 523 times a day.

my fave thing about this kubecon is the hallway track and the ppl im chatting with 

my least fave thing about this kubecon is the extensive and pervasive of AI talks is starting to depress me
I also authored this, so taking #KubeCon questions about it too

#CloudNativeTechnologiesevolving fast and this week it’s happening at KubeCon Europe in Amsterdam. Álvaro Vázquez, Juan Poton and Carlos Giraldo are there exploring the future of cloud-native systems, from Kubernetes to edge-cloud continuum. 

#CPUGPU #FPGA #NeuromorficEdgeComputing

Impactful keynote from Fabian Steinbach about how Zeiss Vision Care built a resilient data extraction processes using Dapr Agents v1.0. This allowed them to build highly deterministic workflows that blend specialized OCR,  LLM calls using the Dapr Conversation API.
#kubecon #agents #ai
Jorge Palma of Microsoft & Natan Yellin of Robusta talk about multiplying SRE effectiveness through CNCF tools like Headlamp, Inspektor Gadget, and HolmesGPT. #KubeCon
